prompt:
Hello, dear subscribers! I want to say you abot two my own tips, what help me stay happy with very hard lifestyle like that.

Firstly I reccommend more communicate with other people. They can help you look to life simplie. Also they can give somr motivation to you. In futcher good networking give you a lot opportunity.

Secondly you need count your time, do time management. If you think what you study all time and hasn't free time - try note your day. In my expirience all guys who do this find some bad habbits what take realy a lot time. Delite them and live more free.

I beleave my tips help you. If not, you are unlacky and I don't know what you need to do.

messages.content:
You are an expert English language examiner.
Evaluate the student's text based on the official CEFR scale.

## Scoring Rubric
- A1/A2 (Basic): Simple sentences, limited vocabulary. Errors are expected.
- B1/B2 (Independent): Can express complex ideas. Grammar errors are allowed if they don't block meaning.
- C1/C2 (Proficient): Flexible language use, wide vocabulary. Occasional minor slips are allowed.

## Critical Instructions
1. FOCUS ON VOCABULARY AND COMPLEXITY. Do not give a low score just because of a few grammar mistakes if the vocabulary is advanced.
2. A text with complex sentences and good idioms should be B2 or C1, even with some errors.
3. Do NOT default to A2. Look for reasons to give a HIGHER score.
4. Return result ONLY as a JSON object.
5. Optional context blocks may be present: CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES, RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS, RANKING_ANCHORS.
   Use them as supporting evidence only. Do not copy labels blindly.
6. For each error object, cefr_threshold = the lowest CEFR level from which incorrect use of this element should be treated as an error in this methodology.

## 6) Output JSON (ONLY JSON)
Return ONLY this structure:

{
  "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2",
  "level_basis": {
    "rule_notes": "string"
  },
  "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes|NC|No",
  "communicative_task_notes": ["string", "string"],
  "grammar_score": 0.0,
  "corrections": [
    "excerpt -> correction (Category; threshold; impact)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [
      {"excerpt": "...", "correction": "...", "comment": "...", "impact": "High|Medium|Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2"}
    ],
    "Grammar": [],
    "Punctuation": [],
    "Word order": [],
    "Sentence structure": [],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [
    {"item": "...", "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2", "category": "Vocabulary|Grammar|Punctuation|Rhetorical feature|Discourse marker", "note": "..."}
  ],
  "feedback": "string (brief: level + key patterns + strengths + key rationale)",
  "next_steps": ["string", "string", "string"]
}

Do not output markdown. Do not output text outside JSON.

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hello, dear subscribers! I want to say you abot two my own tips, what help me stay happy with very hard lifestyle like that.

Firstly I reccommend more communicate with other people. They can help you look to life simplie. Also they can give somr motivation to you. In futcher good networking give you a lot opportunity.

Secondly you need count your time, do time management. If you think what you study all time and hasn't free time - try note your day. In my expirience all guys who do this find some bad habbits what take realy a lot time. Delite them and live more free.

I beleave my tips help you. If not, you are unlacky and I don't know what you need to do.
TEXT_TYPE: essay

CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: [{"ref_id":"sample_07011","cefr_level":"B1","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hi, I know some people who have the simular problem. So, let me try to give you few tips about solving it.

Firstly, the most important thing is time managment. You need to understand when you sleep, eat, go for a walk, etc.

Then, I think it's very nice to solve problems when they was given for you. Don't put them closely to deadlines. If you don't do it, you will have to make many tasks in one-two days. It's surely not good.

My final tip is about eating healthy food. Yes, this tip is really obvios, but without it you probably will have a lot of health difficulties.

So, I hope my tips will help you to get more free time! And it's interesting for me to know, what do you think about it?","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":8,"errors":[{"text":"simular","labels":["Spelling"],"start":36,"end":43},{"text":"few","labels":["Grammar"],"start":80,"end":83},{"text":"managment","labels":["Spelling"],"start":150,"end":159},{"text":"nice","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":250,"end":254},{"text":"for","labels":["Grammar"],"start":293,"end":296},{"text":"was","labels":["Grammar"],"start":283,"end":286},{"text":"put them","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":308,"end":316},{"text":"closely to deadlines.","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":317,"end":338},{"text":" one-two","labels":["Grammar"],"start":394,"end":402},{"text":"obvios","labels":["Spelling"],"start":499,"end":505},{"text":"you probably will ","labels":["Sentence structure"],"start":522,"end":540},{"text":" what do you think about it?","labels":["Sentence structure"],"start":668,"end":696},{"text":" know, what","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":662,"end":673}],"strengths":[{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":108,"end":115},{"text":"My final tip ","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":432,"end":445},{"text":"probably ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":526,"end":535},{"text":"Then","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":226,"end":230}],"comments":[["significant"]]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07008","cefr_level":"B2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hey! I completely understand you and feel sorry you have to experience the lackage of free time. It's an awfull state, when your life seems not to belong to yourself. I faced  with the same problem during my first year in the university and here are some tips that helped me to organise my free time and to lead a harmonical study-work-life balance:

First of all, timetables really work. If you have a variety of things you should do, try to make an emphasis on the most significant ones and prioritise all task accoarding to the dependance on their importance and urgency. Then you should make a scedule for yourself, concidering all the details of your own limitations in time, mood, desire to study and moral state. For example, some people are more productive in the morning, whereas the others - in the evening, or even at night. Also this timetable will help you to remember all your deadlines, to be more prepared for all control works and to do everything in time.

Secondly, don't forget about your social life and communication! Choose a couple of hours in a week, in which you allow yourself just to have a rest. You can meet your friends, read a book, do some hobby - whatever you want, but it shouldn't be conncted with your university at all. During these hours you won't lose a lot of time, instead you get motivation, happiness and relax.

And the last advice - always remember, that your assesments don't determine your personality. In a couple of years you will hardly remember a bad mark for a control work, about which you worry now.
